name: Beans and Sausage (Usda)
recipeInstructions:
A No. 10 can is the equivalent of 7 (16-oz) cans of kidney beans or 4 (28-oz) cans.
1. Line four 8x8-inch baking pans with heat-resistant freezer wrap. Allow enough extra wrap to fold over top. Use one pan for each six servings or one-fourth of the recipe. Do not line pans for food to be served without freezing.
2. Crumble sausage and brown it with onion and pepper flakes in a large skillet, draining off excess fat.
3. Add beans, tomatoes, salt, chili powder, garlic powder, flour, and water to the skillet. Stir until smooth.
4. Simmer the mixture about 30 minutes or until thickened, stirring occasionally.
5. To Serve Without Freezing: Serve hot mixture over cooked rice.
6. To Freeze: Pour each part of the dish into a baking pan. Pack food tightly to avoid air pockets. Cool for 30 minutes at room temperature. Wrap food securely with freezer wrap, folding edges together several times so they lie directly on top of the food. Fold ends of wrap over and seal with freezer tape. Label with name, date frozen, and best-by date (about 4 months). Freeze for 10 to 12 hours before removing packages from pans.
7. To Heat Frozen Food: Preheat oven to 400°F. Remove freezer wrap and place the food in a baking pan. Bake for 1 1/2 hours or until bubbling at edges and hot throughout. Serve over cooked rice.
